topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up on my phone screen, bright and intrusive. It was a direct message from Mr. Harrison. My heart gave a sudden, uncomfortable lurch in my chest. I quickly minimized the app, trying to focus on the textbook spread across my desk, but my eyes kept darting toward the device. He had tagged me in a recent post about a school event—a picture of me, slightly awkward, laughing too loudly. A flush crept up my neck, warming my cheeks instantly. I felt a desperate urge to shove my phone deep into my backpack, wishing the floor would swallow me whole. The silence in the classroom suddenly felt too loud, magnifying the frantic drumming of my pulse.

[story 2]

I was scrolling through my feed, mindlessly flicking through updates, when I saw it: a photo of me from the history presentation, captioned with a comment from Mr. Harrison. My breath hitched. I froze, my fingers hovering over the screen. A strange heat bloomed in my face, making the skin feel tight and prickly. I could feel the attention of the entire room, even though everyone else seemed absorbed in their notes. I quickly switched the screen to black, my hands trembling slightly as I tried to steady them. I mumbled something incoherent about needing water, hoping the sudden movement would distract everyone from the sudden, intense awareness of being observed.

[story 3]

It happened during lunch break. I was trying to eat my sandwich in relative solitude, but my phone buzzed insistently. It was a reply from Mr. Harrison to a story I had briefly posted about a weekend trip. My stomach tightened into a knot. I stared at the screen, unable to look away. A wave of intense self-consciousness washed over me, making my posture stiff. I felt my ears growing hot, and I instinctively pulled my hoodie further over my head, wishing I could become invisible. When I finally looked up, I saw him across the cafeteria, and the brief, charged contact of our eyes sent a visible tremor through my hands.
